title: Failed to add podman to systemd sandbox cgroup
body:
Ubuntu 20.04.2 with podman 3.2.3
when running the command "podman --log-level debug info"

There is a debug error that always causes the system to pause.
DEBU[0240] Failed to add podman to systemd sandbox cgroup: Failed to activate service 'org.freedesktop.systemd1': timed out (service_start_timeout=120000ms).

chaasmadmin@maestro-puppyfood:~$ echo $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR
/run/user/5000

Anytime I try to run a container, similar issues are present with the sandbox cgroup failure. For example when building k0s...
docker build --rm -t k0sbuild.docker-image.k0s -f build/Dockerfile .
STEP 1: FROM golang:1.16-alpine
STEP 2: RUN apk add --no-cache gcc musl-dev
error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c apk add --no-cache gcc musl-dev]: sd-bus call: Connection timed out
: exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN apk add --no-cache gcc musl-dev": error while running runtime: exit status 1
WARN[0480] Failed to add pause process to systemd sandbox cgroup: `<nil>`

Below is the full output of the podman info:

comments.body: @smijolovic it seems like systemd session is not valid , does `systemd-loginctl list-sessions` lists a session for your user `5000`. Could you try `systemctl restart systemd-logind` and run podman info again.

chaasmadmin@maestro-puppyfood:~$ loginctl list-sessions
SESSION  UID USER        SEAT TTY  
      1 5000 chaasmadmin      pts/0
      3 5000 chaasmadmin      pts/1

2 sessions listed.

Same issue persists
@smijolovic could you paste output of `busctl` please and check if `org.freedesktop.systemd1` is `activable` if not try running `sudo systemctl daemon-reexec` or just `systemctl daemon-reexec`

chaasmadmin@maestro-puppyfood:~$ busctl
NAME                            PID PROCESS         USER            CONNECTION    UNIT                        SESSION DESCRIPTION
:1.1                            494 systemd-network systemd-network :1.1          systemd-networkd.service    -       -          
:1.11                           839 unattended-upgr root            :1.11         unattended-upgrades.service -       -          
:1.14                          1448 systemd         chaasmadmin     :1.14         user@5000.service           -       -          
:1.2                            698 systemd-resolve systemd-resolve :1.2          systemd-resolved.service    -       -          
:1.3                            734 systemd-logind  root            :1.3          systemd-logind.service      -       -          
:1.4                            736 usbguard-dbus   root            :1.4          usbguard-dbus.service       -       -          
:1.5                            724 accounts-daemon root            :1.5          accounts-daemon.service     -       -          
:1.7                            877 polkitd         root            :1.7          polkit.service              -       -          
:1.8                            730 networkd-dispat root            :1.8          networkd-dispatcher.service -       -          
:1.86                         68616 salt-call       root            :1.86         chaasm.run.service          -       -          
:1.90                             1 systemd         root            :1.90         init.scope                  -       -          
:1.91                         69937 busctl          chaasmadmin     :1.91         session-16.scope            16      -

com.ubuntu.SoftwareProperties     - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
io.netplan.Netplan                - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
org.freedesktop.Accounts        724 accounts-daemon root            :1.5          accounts-daemon.service     -       -          
org.freedesktop.DBus              1 systemd         root            -             init.scope                  -       -          
org.freedesktop.PackageKit        - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
org.freedesktop.PolicyKit1      877 polkitd         root            :1.7          polkit.service              -       -          
org.freedesktop.UPower            - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
org.freedesktop.hostname1         - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
org.freedesktop.locale1           - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -

org.freedesktop.network1        494 systemd-network systemd-network :1.1          systemd-networkd.service    -       -          
org.freedesktop.resolve1        698 systemd-resolve systemd-resolve :1.2          systemd-resolved.service    -       -          
org.freedesktop.systemd1          1 systemd         root            :1.90         init.scope                  -       -          
org.freedesktop.thermald          - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
org.freedesktop.timedate1         - -               -               (activatable) -                           -       -          
org.usbguard1                   736 usbguard-dbus   root            :1.4          usbguard-dbus.service       -       -

A systemctl daemon-reexec is not making it activatable

@smijolovic I am not sure why is it not active so `org.freedesktop.systemd1` needs to be activatable.

What does `systemctl status org.freedesktop.systemd1` says ?

Some common knows issues are

* Low memory or swap. Check if you are running low on memory. Clean up process if its low and then try `sudo systemctl daemon-reexec`
* Try restarting dbus `systemctl start dbus.service`
* This i am not sure about but found somewhere on web. `sudo kill 1`.
* Last resort if you could `reboot`.

What's really strange is that my other system is working...and shows the same systemd1 result as this one, so I'm pretty sure that is not the issue. One has selinux permissive and the other is enforcing.  When I run setenforce 0 to the second system, it still doesn't work....so I'm stumped on why they are behaving differently.

Memory is not the issue - 12G allocated, 11G free.  The working system only has 8GB allocated.

I will close this issue for now - because it is an selinux related issue - once set to permissive at boot - podman and k0s build.

More work to be done on the policies to address this.  Thank you for all of your help!

I see the following with ausearch, but audit2allow does not supply a policy to address the issue:

time->Fri Aug 20 19:19:09 2021
node=maestro-puppyfood type=PROCTITLE msg=audit(1629487149.823:1023368): proctitle=2F6C69622F73797374656D642F73797374656D64002D2D75736572
node=maestro-puppyfood type=PATH msg=audit(1629487149.823:1023368): item=0 name="/proc/1800/cgroup" nametype=UNKNOWN cap_fp=0 cap_fi=0 cap_fe=0 cap_fver=0 cap_frootid=0
node=maestro-puppyfood type=SYSCALL msg=audit(1629487149.823:1023368): arch=c000003e syscall=257 success=no exit=-13 a0=ffffff9c a1=7ffcc8c99110 a2=80000 a3=0 items=1 ppid=1 pid=1719 auid=5000 uid=5000 gid=5000 euid=5000 suid=5000 fsuid=5000 egid=5000 sgid=5000 fsgid=5000 tty=(none) ses=2 comm="systemd" exe="/usr/lib/systemd/systemd" subj=sysadm_u:sysadm_r:sysadm_systemd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 key="perm_access"
node=maestro-puppyfood type=AVC msg=audit(1629487149.823:1023368): avc:  denied  { search } for  pid=1719 comm="systemd" name="1800" dev="proc" ino=1460330 scontext=sysadm_u:sysadm_r:sysadm_systemd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=sysadm_u:sysadm_r:sysadm_dbusd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tclass=dir permissive=0

chaasmadmin@maestro-puppyfood:~$ journalctl -t setroubleshoot
-- Logs begin at Fri 2021-08-20 19:27:50 UTC, end at Fri 2021-08-20 19:39:49 UTC. --
-- No entries --

The AVC is visible in the search....but setroubleshoot is not seeing it.  Any suggestions there?
sysadmin_systemd_t is not defined in default policy on Fedora,  so this looks like it is custom policy and outside of Podmans control.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Host policy cannot be changed by podman. Closing this as `WONTFIX` please reopen if you feel issue is with podman.
